title: Phase 10b sidecar — mitigation splits + additions
description: Phase-10b LLM persists at PHASE_END. Refines the Python aggregator's 1:1 mitigation grouping with semantic splits (one M-ID into multiple distinct mitigations when remediation steps span different OWASP categories) and additions (process / cross-cutting mitigations not derivable from any single threat — e.g. 'Establish dependency-update SLA', 'Adopt centralized secrets manager').

splits.description: Splits an existing baseline M-ID into multiple finer-grained mitigations. Union of split.threat_ids[] MUST equal source M-ID's threat_ids[] (no dropped threats — aggregator validates).

source_mid.description: M-ID present in the Python baseline. Cross-ref validated — orphan source_mid = hard fail.

into.description: ≥2 sub-mitigations. Aggregator assigns final ID = source_mid + id_suffix (M-001 + 'a' = M-001a).

id_suffix.description: Lowercase letter (a, b, c, ...) appended to source_mid. Must be unique within this split's into[].

additions.description: Cross-cutting / process mitigations not derivable from threats[]. Each M-ID MUST come from scripts/reserve_ids.py mitigation --count N.

id.description: Reserved via scripts/reserve_ids.py. Aggregator validates ID ≤ current counter AND not colliding with baseline.

threat_ids.description: ≥1 existing T-ID required (evidence-grounding — no hallucinated mitigations). Process mitigations (kind=process) SHOULD have ≥2.

kind.description: fix = concrete code/config change; review = manual code review; process = SDLC/operational gap; architectural = design-level change.

priority.description: Optional. Defaults to derive-from-severity in the aggregator (P1=Critical, P2=High, P3=Medium, P4=Low).

severity.description: Optional. Aggregator defaults to 'Medium' when omitted — LLM consistently forgets severity for process mitigations, so this is forgiving by design.
